questions.q: Of which kingdom is Rama, hero of the Ramayana, the prince?
questions.explanation: Rama is the prince of Ayodhya, the capital of the Kosala kingdom ruled by the Ikshvaku dynasty.

questions.q: Rama is traditionally counted as which numbered avatar of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Rama is revered as the seventh of Vishnu's ten principal avatars, the Dashavatara.

questions.q: Who is the wife of Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sita, daughter of King Janaka and an incarnation of Lakshmi, is the wife of Rama.

questions.q: Which demon-king of Lanka abducts Sita, the wife of Rama?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the ten-headed rakshasa king of Lanka, abducts Sita, setting the central conflict of the Ramayana in motion.

questions.q: Which devoted vanara is Rama's greatest helper in the search for Sita?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, son of the wind-god Vayu, is the supreme devotee of Rama and his greatest ally in finding and rescuing Sita.

questions.q: Which sage is traditionally credited with composing the Ramayana, the epic of Rama?
questions.explanation: The sage Valmiki, honored as the adi-kavi or first poet, is traditionally credited with composing the Ramayana.

questions.q: What is the chief weapon associated with Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama is renowned as a great archer, and the bow is his emblem and chief weapon.

questions.q: Honored as Maryada Purushottama, Rama is celebrated as the perfect embodiment of what principle?
questions.explanation: Rama is revered as Maryada Purushottama, the ideal man and perfect embodiment of dharma, or righteousness.

questions.q: To what setting is Rama sent for many years before the events of his return as king?
questions.explanation: Rama is exiled to live in the forest, a key episode that shapes the narrative of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which brother of Rama accompanies him faithfully into forest exile in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, Rama's devoted brother, renounces home to share the forest exile and serve Rama and Sita.

questions.q: What is the name of the great bow wielded by Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama's bow is named Kodanda, and he is sometimes called Kodandarama for wielding it.

questions.q: Who is the father of Rama, the king of Ayodhya in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama is the son of King Dasharatha, the Ikshvaku ruler of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Whose insistence on a boon from Dasharatha brings about Rama's exile to the forest?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi demands the boons that send Rama into fourteen years of forest exile and place her son Bharata on the throne.

questions.q: How does Rama win the hand of Sita at the swayamvara in Mithila?
options: By defeating a thousand kings in single combat
By lifting and stringing Shiva's mighty bow
By answering the riddles of King Janaka
By retrieving a celestial flower from heaven
questions.explanation: Rama wins Sita by lifting and stringing the mighty bow of Shiva, which no other suitor could even move.

questions.q: Which queen is the mother of Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kausalya, the chief queen of Dasharatha, is the mother of Rama.

questions.q: Which sage takes the young Rama and Lakshmana to protect his sacrifice and slay the demoness Tataka?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishwamitra leads the young Rama and Lakshmana to guard his sacrifice and slay the demoness Tataka.

questions.q: Which vanara king becomes Rama's ally after Rama helps him regain his kingdom of Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Rama allies with the vanara Sugriva, helping him reclaim Kishkindha, and Sugriva's army then aids in the search for Sita.

questions.q: What great structure does Rama's army build to cross the sea to Lanka?
options: A bridge or causeway across the sea
questions.explanation: In the Yuddha Kanda, the vanara army builds the causeway known as Rama Setu across the sea to Lanka.

questions.q: After Ravana's defeat, whom does Rama consecrate as the new king of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama crowns Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous younger brother who had joined his cause, as the new king of Lanka.

questions.q: The noble vulture Jatayu, mortally wounded by Ravana, performs what service for Rama?
options: He builds the bridge to Lanka
He carries Rama across the ocean
He reveals the direction of Sita's captivity
questions.explanation: Jatayu, dying after trying to stop Ravana, lives long enough to tell Rama the direction in which Sita was carried away.

questions.q: On what flying chariot do Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana return to Ayodhya after the war?
questions.explanation: Rama and his companions return to Ayodhya aboard the Pushpaka Vimana, the flying chariot reclaimed after Ravana's fall.

questions.q: Into how many books, or kandas, is Valmiki's Ramayana traditionally divided?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana is traditionally arranged in seven kandas, or books.

questions.q: Which dynasty, descended from the line of the sun, does Rama belong to in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama belongs to the Ikshvaku dynasty of the solar line (Suryavamsha), descended from Surya through Ikshvaku.

questions.q: Which brother rules Ayodhya in Rama's absence, placing Rama's sandals on the throne as a token of his authority?
questions.explanation: Bharata refuses the throne for himself and rules as regent in Rama's name, enshrining Rama's sandals (paduka) upon it.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what are the names of the twin sons born to Rama and Sita?
questions.explanation: Lava and Kusha are the twin sons of Rama and Sita, raised in Valmiki's hermitage in the Uttara Kanda.

questions.q: Which rakshasi's disfigurement by Lakshmana ignites the conflict that leads to Sita's abduction?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha, Ravana's sister, is disfigured after pursuing Rama and Lakshmana, and her complaint prompts Ravana to abduct Sita.

questions.q: What golden form does the demon Maricha take to lure Rama away from Sita's side?
questions.explanation: At Ravana's bidding, Maricha takes the form of a golden deer to draw Rama away so that Sita can be abducted.

questions.q: Which sage of the south blesses Rama in the forest and gifts him celestial weapons in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The sage Agastya, who carried Vedic culture to the south, blesses Rama and bestows celestial weapons upon him in the forest.

questions.q: Whom does Rama free from a curse, restoring her from stone to her form, on the way to Mithila?
questions.explanation: Rama redeems Ahalya, the wife of sage Gautama, releasing her from the curse that had turned her to stone.

questions.q: What is the name of the elder vanara king whom Rama slays to restore the throne of Kishkindha to Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Rama slays Vali, the powerful vanara king, so that his brother Sugriva can regain the kingdom of Kishkindha.
